title: oenothera fruticosa var. subglobosa
intro: Oenothera fruticosa var. subglobosa, also known as the Fringed Evening Primrose, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its delicate, pinkish-purple flowers and small, ovate leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oenothera fruticosa var. subglobosa include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for skin conditions and wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
